CVE-2002-2439.title: Integer overflow in the new[] operator in gcc before 4.8.0 allows ...
CVE-2002-2439.description: Integer overflow in the new[] operator in gcc before 4.8.0 allows attackers to have unspecified impacts.

CVE-2016-2226.title: Integer overflow in the string_appends function in cplus-dem.c in ...
CVE-2016-2226.description: Integer overflow in the string_appends function in cplus-dem.c in libiberty allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted executable, which triggers a buffer overflow.

CVE-2016-4487.title: Use-after-free vulnerability in libiberty allows remote attackers ...
CVE-2016-4487.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary, related to "btypevec."

CVE-2016-4488.title: Use-after-free vulnerability in libiberty allows remote attackers ...
CVE-2016-4488.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary, related to "ktypevec."

CVE-2016-4489.title: Integer overflow in the gnu_special function in libiberty allows  ...
CVE-2016-4489.description: Integer overflow in the gnu_special function in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary, related to the "demangling of virtual tables."

CVE-2016-4490.title: Integer overflow in cp-demangle.c in libiberty allows remote atta ...
CVE-2016-4490.description: Integer overflow in cp-demangle.c in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary, related to inconsistent use of the long and int types for lengths.

CVE-2016-4491.title: The d_print_comp function in cp-demangle.c in libiberty allows re ...
CVE-2016-4491.description: The d_print_comp function in cp-demangle.c in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary, which triggers infinite recursion and a buffer overflow, related to a node having "itself as ancestor more than once."

CVE-2016-4492.title: Buffer overflow in the do_type function in cplus-dem.c in libiber ...
CVE-2016-4492.description: Buffer overflow in the do_type function in cplus-dem.c in libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and crash) via a crafted binary.

CVE-2016-4493.title: The demangle_template_value_parm and do_hpacc_template_literal fu ...
CVE-2016-4493.description: The demangle_template_value_parm and do_hpacc_template_literal functions in cplus-dem.c in libiberty allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and crash) via a crafted binary.

CVE-2016-6131.title: The demangler in GNU Libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a ...
CVE-2016-6131.description: The demangler in GNU Libiberty allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (infinite loop, stack overflow, and crash) via a cycle in the references of remembered mangled types.

CVE-2017-11671.title: Under certain circumstances, the ix86_expand_builtin function in  ...
CVE-2017-11671.description: Under certain circumstances, the ix86_expand_builtin function in i386.c in GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) version 4.6, 4.7, 4.8, 4.9, 5 before 5.5, and 6 before 6.4 will generate instruction sequences that clobber the status flag of the RDRAND and RDSEED intrinsics before it can be read, potentially causing failures of these instructions to go unreported. This could potentially lead to less randomness in random number generation.

CVE-2020-35536.title: Rejected reason: DO NOT USE THIS CANDIDATE NUMBER. ConsultIDs: no ...
CVE-2020-35536.description: Rejected reason: DO NOT USE THIS CANDIDATE NUMBER. ConsultIDs: none. Reason: This candidate was withdrawn by its CNA. Further investigation showed that it was not a security issue. Notes: none.

CVE-2022-27943.title: libiberty/rust-demangle.c in GNU GCC 11.2 allows stack consumptio ...
CVE-2022-27943.description: libiberty/rust-demangle.c in GNU GCC 11.2 allows stack consumption in demangle_const, as demonstrated by nm-new.

CVE-2023-4039.title: **DISPUTED**A failure in the -fstack-protector feature in GCC-bas ...
CVE-2023-4039.description:
**DISPUTED**A failure in the -fstack-protector feature in GCC-based toolchains 
that target AArch64 allows an attacker to exploit an existing buffer 
overflow in dynamically-sized local variables in your application 
without this being detected. This stack-protector failure only applies 
to C99-style dynamically-sized local variables or those created using 
alloca(). The stack-protector operates as intended for statically-sized 
local variables.

The default behavior when the stack-protector 
detects an overflow is to terminate your application, resulting in 
controlled loss of availability. An attacker who can exploit a buffer 
overflow without triggering the stack-protector might be able to change 
program flow control to cause an uncontrolled loss of availability or to
 go further and affect confidentiality or integrity. NOTE: The GCC project argues that this is a missed hardening bug and not a vulnerability by itself.
